configuration-element.name: RuntimeState
configuration-element.description: Runtime state for the Adaptive Snapshot Cleanup workflow. Holds the distributed mutex lock (runLock). If a workflow run terminates abnormally without releasing this lock, clear it manually by setting runLock to an empty string before triggering the next run.

attribute.name: runLock
attribute.description: Active run ID when a run is in progress (format: SCR-YYYY-MM-DDTHH-MM-SS). Empty string when no run is active. WARNING: Never edit this value while a run is confirmed to be active. To clear a stuck lock: set this to empty string and save.

attribute.name: lastRunId
attribute.description: Run ID of the last completed or aborted run. Informational only. Updated automatically at the end of each run.

attribute.name: lastRunCompletedAt
attribute.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the last run completion. Informational only.

attribute.name: lastRunOutcome
attribute.description: Outcome of the last run: SUCCESS | DRY_RUN_COMPLETE | COMPLETED_WITH_ERRORS | COMPLETED_WITH_DEFERRALS | CLEAN_EXIT | MUTEX_ABORT | ERROR. Informational only.

configuration-element.name: Thresholds
configuration-element.description: Default governor thresholds and execution parameters for the Adaptive Snapshot Cleanup workflow. Used as defaults for scheduled runs. All values are overridable via the workflow input form on on-demand runs. Tune latencyThresholdMs and vsanCongestionThresh based on observed baseline storage performance in your environment.

attribute.name: maxAgeMinutes
attribute.description: Snapshots older than this many minutes are candidates for deletion. TESTING : set to 5-60 minutes to catch recently created test snapshots. PRODUCTION: set to 10080 (7 days) or as required by policy. This value does NOT cause automatic deletion — the workflow must still be triggered manually or via schedule.

attribute.name: nameMatchString
attribute.description: Whitelist name filter. When non-empty, ONLY snapshots whose names contain this string (case-insensitive) are eligible. Leave empty to target all snapshots regardless of name. Example: 'pre-patch' — only deletes snapshots named 'pre-patch', 'Pre-Patch-2026-04-10', 'PREPATCH', etc.

attribute.name: descIgnoreString
attribute.description: Description ignore filter. Snapshots whose description field contains this string (case-insensitive) are skipped individually. The rest of the VM's snapshots are still eligible. Example: 'do not delete' — protects snapshots tagged with that phrase.

attribute.name: dryRunDefault
attribute.description: Default dry-run state for scheduled runs. When true, no snapshots are deleted — all operations are simulated and logged. Set to false only after validating dry-run output. IMPORTANT: The workflow input form always shows this as a visible field so operators can override it per-run.

attribute.name: latencyThresholdMs
attribute.description: VMFS and NFS datastores: maximum projected total I/O latency in milliseconds before the governor holds the next consolidation. The governor projects: current_latency + observed_delta_from_last_task. If the projection exceeds this value, the task is held and the governor polls until conditions improve. TUNING: set to approximately 2x your observed idle latency during the cleanup window. If idle latency is 12ms, set this to 24-30ms. All-flash environments: 8-15ms. Spinning disk: 20-40ms.

attribute.name: vsanCongestionThresh
attribute.description: vSAN datastores: maximum projected vSAN congestion score (0-255). vSAN reports congestion as an integer; values above 128 indicate serious congestion affecting all VMs on the cluster. The governor projects: current_congestion + observed_delta. TUNING: 30-50 = conservative (fewer concurrent cleanups allowed). 80-100 = moderate. Above 128 = permissive (not recommended). Default 50 provides a safe margin below the serious-congestion threshold.

attribute.name: vsanResyncThresholdGB
attribute.description: vSAN datastores: maximum projected resync queue depth in gigabytes. vSAN resync activity competes with guest I/O for storage bandwidth. A large resync queue (>10GB) indicates significant background rebalancing is in progress — adding consolidation I/O at this point risks guest performance degradation. TUNING: 5GB = conservative. 10GB = default. 20GB = permissive.

attribute.name: maxParallelPerVcenter
attribute.description: Maximum number of simultaneous snapshot consolidation tasks per vCenter for powered-on VMs. Powered-off VMs do not count against this limit but still count against the I/O governor. vCenter VPXD default task queue: 35 tasks. TUNING: 3 = conservative default. Raise to 5-8 only after validating that the governor correctly limits I/O impact. Do not exceed 10 without explicit vCenter task queue analysis.

attribute.name: governorPollIntervalSec
attribute.description: How often (in seconds) the adaptive I/O governor re-evaluates datastore metrics when a consolidation task is on hold. Lower values = more responsive but higher API call frequency. Higher values = less responsive but fewer performance manager queries. RECOMMENDED: 30 seconds for most environments.

attribute.name: taskTimeoutSeconds
attribute.description: Maximum time in seconds to wait for a single vCenter snapshot removal task to reach a success or error state before treating it as a timeout failure. Deep snapshot chains (10+ snapshots) can require longer consolidation. Thin-provisioned disks on heavily loaded storage may also be slow. RECOMMENDED: 1800 (30 minutes). Raise to 3600 for environments with known slow storage or very deep snapshot chains.
